questions.question: Who allocates and reshuffles various portfolios among the ministers?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister of India allocates and reshuffles various portfolios among the ministers. He can also ask a minister to resign or advise the President to dismiss him in case of difference of opinion.

questions.question: Which amendment act of Parliament increased the strength of Lok Sabha from 525 to 545?
questions.explanation: The strength of Lok Sabha was increased from 525 to 545 by the 31st Constitutional Amendment Act of 1973. The number of members to represent the states was increased from 500 to 525 and number of members to represent the UTs was decreased from 25 to 20.

questions.question: Which year is related to the formation of Rajya Sabha for the first time?
questions.explanation: The Rajya Sabha was constituted on 3rd April, 1952 for the first time. The Article 80 lays down that the maximum strength of Rajya Sabha should be 250 out of which 12 are to be nominated by the President.

questions.question: What can a President do when a money bill is presented to him?1. Return the bill2. Reject the bill3. Give assent to the bill4. Recommend changes to the billSelect the correct option from the codes given below:
questions.explanation: When a money bill is presented to the president he may either give his assent to the bill or withhold his assent to the bill.He cannot return the bill for reconsideration of the Houses of the Parliament.

questions.question: What is the judgement of I.R. Coelho case (2007) on the power of judicial review?

options.label: A
options.text: Immunity from judicial review for acts under Ninth Schedule

options.label: B
options.text: No blanket immunity from judicial review for acts under Ninth Schedule

options.label: C
options.text: Judicial review is not a basic feature of Constitution

answer.label: B
answer.text: No blanket immunity from judicial review for acts under Ninth Schedule

questions.explanation: In I.R. Coelho case (2007) , the Supreme Court ruled that laws falling under the Ninth Schedule can not have any blanket immunity from judicial review. Supreme Court further opined that judicial review is a ‘basic feature’ of the constitution and cannot be taken away by even using Ninth Schedule.

questions.question: Who can make extraterritorial legislation among the following?
questions.explanation: The Parliament of the India is alone empowered to make ‘extraterritorial legislation’. It means that the laws of the Parliament are also applicable to the Indian citizens and their property in any part of the world.

questions.question: Which of the following subjects were transferred to Concurrent List from State List by 42nd Amendment Act of 1976?
questions.explanation: Five subjects were transferred to Concurrent List from State List, that is, (a) education, (b) forests, (c) weights and measures, (d) protection of wild animals and birds, and (e) administration of justice; constitution and organisation of all courts except the Supreme Court and the high courts by the 42nd Amendment Act of 1976.

questions.question: In which year was the first proclamation of National Emergency issued because of Chinese aggression in the NEFA?
questions.explanation: The first proclamation of National Emergency by the President was issued in October 1962 on account of Chinese aggression in the NEFA (North-East Frontier Agency–now Arunachal Pradesh). It remained in force till January 1968.

questions.question: In how many states the government headed by Morarji Desai imposed President’s Rule in 1977?
questions.explanation: In 1977 after the internal emergency, the ruling Congress Party lost and the Janta Party came to power. The then Prime Minister Morarji Desai imposed President’s Rule in nine states (where the Congress Party was in power) on the ground that the assemblies in those states no longer represented the wishes of the electorate.

questions.question: Which of the following bills the Governor cannot return back for the reconsideration of the state legislature?
questions.explanation: After a bill is passed by the state legislature and sent to the Governor, he can return the bill for the reconsideration of the state legislature except the money bill.
